messages.content: Create a musical scale in scala format for a temperament that has 20 notes seperated by 7-limit just intervals

There are different dispositions of 20-note just intonation, but here is one possibility:

! 20-note 7-limit Just Scale
!
20
!
49/48
7/6
21/16
4/3
441/320
3/2
7/4
441/256
2/1
320/147
147/98
98/63
63/40
40/27
27/16
441/256
16/9
294/167
21/10
10/3

In this scale, each frequency ratio is a multiple of powers of the primes 2, 3, and 7, with the largest prime factor being 7. The smallest interval is a septimal comma of 49/48, and the largest is a septimal tritone of 10/3. Note that 7/6 is not tempered as a equal-tempered minor seventh, but as a septimal subminor seventh. Similarly, 441/320 is not an equal-tempered augmented fifth, but a septimal supermajor fifth. This results in a unique sound with more pure and complex harmonies than those possible in equal temperament.
create a musical scale in scala format that approximates 12-tone equal temperament but has a few notes that better approximate 7-limit just intonation or 31-tone equal tempermant

Here's a 12-tone equal temperament scale with a few notes adjusted to better approximate just intonation and 31-tone equal temperament:

! 12-tone ET scale with JI and 31-TET adjustments
!
12
!
1/1
16/15
9/8
6/5
5/4
4/3
7/5
3/2
8/5
5/3
7/4
15/8

The first 6 notes are standard 12-tone equal temperament intervals, but the next 6 notes have been adjusted. The 4th note is lowered slightly to approximate a pure just minor third (6/5), and the 7th note is raised slightly to approximate a pure just subminor seventh (7/5). The 8th note is raised slightly to approximate a just harmonic seventh (15/8), and the 11th note is lowered to approximate a pure just fourth (4/3). This creates a scale that has a slightly different sound than standard 12-tone equal temperament. Additionally, this scale can be seen as an approximate subset of 31-tone equal temperament, which divides the octave into 31 equally spaced intervals.
Create a short wikipedia article in source format about doro wat

{{infobox prepared food
| name = Doro Wat
| image = Doro wot.JPG
| caption = A plate of doro wat served with injera
| country = Ethiopia
| region = Horn of Africa
| type = Stew
| mainingredient = Chicken, Berbere, onions, garlic, ginger, butter, niter kibbeh, eggs
| variations =
| servingtemperature = Hot
| calories =
| other = }}

'''Doro wat''' is a spicy Ethiopian stew made with chicken and flavored with a blend of spices called '''berbere'''. It is one of the most popular dishes in Ethiopian cuisine and is often served during holidays and special occasions.

== Ingredients and preparation ==
Doro wat is made with chicken, onions, garlic, ginger, and a blend of spices called berbere. Berbere typically includes chili peppers, garlic, ginger, coriander, cinnamon, cardamom, fenugreek, and cloves. The chicken is typically stewed in a tomato-based sauce and is often served with a boiled egg.

To make doro wat, chicken pieces are marinated in a mixture of lemon juice and salt, then sautéed with a mix of onions, garlic, and ginger. The berbere seasoning is then added along with tomatoes and water, and the chicken is simmered until cooked through. A few hard-boiled eggs are often added to the stew just before serving.

== Serving ==
Doro wat is typically served with a traditional Ethiopian bread called '''injera''', which is used to scoop up the stew. Injera is a sourdough flatbread made with teff flour and has a slightly spongy texture that makes it perfect for soaking up sauces.

Doro wat is often served during holidays and special occasions such as Easter, Christmas, and weddings. It is also a popular dish for family gatherings and dinner parties.

== See also ==
* [[List of stews]]

== External links ==

What unique beasts, monsters, creatures, and items does Dwarf Fortress have that other games and franchises don't have?

Dwarf Fortress has many unique beasts, monsters, creatures, and items that set it apart from other games and franchises. Here are a few examples:

1. Forgotten beasts: These are creatures that are randomly generated for each world, and each one is unique. They have strange abilities, such as the ability to breathe fire or to turn other creatures to stone. They are often quite powerful and can pose a significant threat to your dwarves.

2. Werebeasts: These are creatures that can transform from a humanoid form into a bestial form. There are many different types of werebeasts, including werewolves, werebears, and werelizards. When a dwarf is bitten by a werebeast, they can also become one themselves.

3. Giant cave spiders: These are spiders that can grow to be the size of a house. They can be found in the deep caverns of your fortress, and can be especially dangerous if your dwarves are not prepared to fight them.

4. Demon portals: These are portals that can appear randomly in your fortress, which demons can use to enter your realm. Demons are extremely powerful creatures that can only be defeated by your most skilled warriors.

5. Magic: Dwarf Fortress has a robust magic system that allows your dwarves to cast spells, summon creatures, and control the elements. However, using magic can be dangerous, as it can attract the attention of powerful demons or bring about strange and unforeseen events.

6. Artifact items: Dwarves can create their own unique artifacts that have special abilities. These items can range from weapons and armor to musical instruments and jewelry. The more skilled the dwarf, the more powerful the artifact.

7. Clever crafting: In Dwarf Fortress, players can craft intricate machines, traps, and defenses to protect their fortress from invading creatures. With the right materials and skills, dwarves can create impressive feats of engineering, such as gears, pressure plates, and drawbridges.

Overall, Dwarf Fortress is a game that offers a rich and complex world full of strange creatures and items, making it unique and compelling for fans of fantasy and simulation games.
What is abstract wikipedia?

Abstract Wikipedia is an initiative proposed by the Wikimedia Foundation, the organization that operates Wikipedia and other Wiki-based projects. The initiative aims to create a new kind of information resource that would complement and enhance the existing Wikipedia encyclopedia.

The idea behind Abstract Wikipedia is to create a common language, or knowledge graph, that can support automated content creation in a wide range of languages. This would involve creating a set of structured data that can be easily translated into natural language descriptions by machine learning algorithms. The end result would be a Wikipedia-like online resource, but one that is entirely generated by machines, rather than by regular Wikipedia contributors.

The idea is motivated by a desire to overcome the limitations of human languages, which can create barriers to knowledge transfer and understanding across different cultures and languages. By creating a common language, or knowledge graph, it is hoped that the initiative could accelerate the spread of knowledge across the world, and make it more accessible to people who might not otherwise be able to access it.

The project is in its early stages, and there is currently no concrete timetable for its implementation. However, the Wikimedia Foundation has expressed a strong commitment to the initiative, and is currently working to develop the necessary technology and algorithms to support it. Ultimately, the goal of Abstract Wikipedia is to create a new kind of knowledge resource that has the power to transform the way we think about and access information online.
what is wikifunctions

WikiFunctions is a project under development by the Wikimedia Foundation. Its aim is to create a platform that allows for the collaborative development of code and algorithms in a wiki-like environment. The platform will provide a way for programmers, developers, and subject matter experts to collaborate and create reusable code and algorithms that can be shared across different projects and platforms, including Wikipedia.

One of the goals of WikiFunctions is to make it easy for developers to create and share small pieces of code, called microfunctions, that can be used to automate various tasks on Wikipedia and other Wikimedia projects. For example, a developer could create a function to automatically translate article titles into multiple languages, or to automatically generate citations for a particular source.

WikiFunctions is being developed as an extension of Wikidata, the structured data repository that underlies many Wikimedia projects. The platform will allow developers to create and test code using Wikidata's rich and structured data, and to collaborate with other developers and subject matter experts through a wiki-like interface.

The project is still in the development phase, and there is currently no official release date for WikiFunctions. However, the Wikimedia Foundation has expressed a strong commitment to the project, and is actively working to develop the necessary technology and infrastructure to support it. Ultimately, the goal of WikiFunctions is to provide a flexible and collaborative platform that can help accelerate the development of new tools and algorithms for the Wikimedia ecosystem and beyond.

in the systematic driving of a fleet of floating palaces 
all round the world at the rate of from twelve to

a comparatively shallow plateau or ridge in the

between Europe and America. The first Atlantic

many vicissitudes at first, as most contrivances do 
in their beginnings, communication between the 
two continents was successfully established. Sound- 
ings taken elsewhere showed that somewhat similar

now the whole of Western Europe is being bound 
more firmly, by additional cables, to the eastern 
sea-bord of America. 

This great and glorious achievement has been the 
result of the discovery of two truths, of a truth in 
science on the one hand, and a truth in regard to 
the structure of the bed of the sea on the other. 
The study of electricity and of deep-sea soundings 
was begun and carried on for the sake of the dis- "

== Results of Deep-Sea Soundings ==

In the course of scientific investigation, great discoveries have been made, discoveries that have led to as yet unrealized results. One such discovery is that of deep-sea sounding. Although still in its infancy, deep-sea sounding has already resulted in the discovery of a comparatively shallow plateau or ridge in the North Atlantic Ocean, rising between Ireland and Newfoundland. This discovery has been turned to practical account, as the plateau has been chosen to be the bed of our electric telegraph between Europe and America. The first Atlantic cable was laid on it, and although it suffered many setbacks initially, communication between the two continents was successfully established. 

Soundings taken elsewhere showed that somewhat similar plateaus existed in other parts of the Atlantic, and now the whole of Western Europe is being bound more firmly to the eastern seaboard of America by additional cables.

This great and glorious achievement has been the result of the discovery of two truths: truth in science on the one hand, and truth in regard to the structure of the bed of the sea on the other. The study of electricity and deep-sea soundings was begun and carried on for the sake of the discovery of these truths. 

As with many discoveries, it is conceivable that the systematic driving of a fleet of floating palaces all around the world at the rate of from twelve to fifteen or twenty miles an hour could result from the inventions predicated on these initial discoveries. The possibilities are endless.
